key_insight: A multimodal pretreatment signature outperformed single biomarkers for predicting primary resistance to anti-PD-(L)1 therapy in advanced NSCLC, but the effect was only moderate.
clinical_relevance: This work suggests that combining clinical, immune, tumor, and molecular features may better stratify patients before immunotherapy than PD-L1 or TMB alone.
method: Prospective multicentre study across 17 centers in advanced NSCLC.
Pretreatment profiling across six layers: clinical, routine labs, circulating immune markers, tumor digital pathology, transcriptomics, and genomics.
Compared 36 feature-selection methods and 10 machine-learning models using .632 optimism correction.
evidence_signal: In 435 evaluable patients, the multimodal model reached an optimism-corrected AUROC of 0.73±0.05 and PPV of 0.63±0.07, versus PPV 0.36 for PD-L1 and 0.35 for TMB.
watch_item: The signature is complex and only moderately predictive, so external validation and practical assay standardization are still needed before routine use.
paper_title: Multimodal profiling for prediction of primary resistance to anti-PD-(L)1 therapy in advanced non-small-cell lung cancer: the prospective PIONeeR biomarkers study
